title: [Arc] Consider array/struct field alignment when computing state size

features.name: struct_field_alignment_padding
features.description: Code should include a struct type with multiple fields whose combined bit widths are not a multiple of 8, requiring byte alignment padding that increases the actual storage size beyond the summed bit width.
features.snippet: typedef struct packed { logic [4:0] tag; logic sign_ext; logic [2:0] offset; logic [1:0] size; } my_struct;

features.name: struct_storage_size_mismatch
features.description: Code should include a struct where the actual allocated storage size exceeds the naive sum of field bit widths due to alignment requirements, simulating the bug where tool A (Arcilator) underestimates memory allocation compared to tool B (Verilator).
features.snippet: typedef struct packed { logic [4:0] tag; logic sign_ext; logic [2:0] offset; logic [1:0] size; } my_struct; // fields: 5+1+3+2=11 bits, but aligned to 16 bits
